chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.1884 of 2023 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs Disposed of matter 1. For orders on M.A. No.4073/2026 (U/A) 2. For orders on M.A. No.2555/2026 (Return of surety) ----------------------------------------------------- 09.03.2026 Mr. Mian Kamran Ahmed, advocate for applicant/surety Mr. Sharafuddin Kanhar, A.P.G. ------------------------------------- Along with urgent application, applicant/surety has moved application for return of surety. It appears that interim pre-arrest bail was granted to applicant Syed Zubair-ul-Haq son of Syed Manzoor ul Haq by this Court vide order dated 28.08.2023, subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Nazir of this Court, which was later on confirmed by this Court vide order dated 26.09.2023. As per Nazir’s endorsement one Muhammad Basil Khan son of Israr Ahmed furnished surety by way of depositing cash amount Rs.50,000/-. Learned counsel applicant/surety submits that applicant/accused above named was acquitted by learned Judicial Magistrate-I, Karachi South vide order dated 27.07.2024. Certified copy of vide order dated 27.07.2024 has been placed on record. He further submits in view of acquittal of the applicant/accused, surety furnished is no more required. Mr. Sharafuddin Kanhar, A.P.G. present in Court in some other cases, waives notice and after having gone through the case file and aforesaid orders has recorded his no objection for return of surety. In view of above, surety furnished by the applicant/ surety is no more required.

Sharafuddin Kanhar, A.P.G. present in Court in some other cases, waives notice and after having gone through the case file and aforesaid orders has recorded his no objection for return of surety. In view of above, surety furnished by the applicant/ surety is no more required. Application for return of surety is allowed. Office is directed to return the surety to the applicant/surety upon proper verification and identification, as per rules. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
